commands.purpose: Red gate for the full coordinator callback executor test suite, written before scripts/coordinator_callback.py existed.
commands.observed_result: 30 failed. Every test failed with FileNotFoundError from importlib.util.spec_from_file_location/exec_module because scripts/coordinator_callback.py did not exist yet -- red for the right reason (missing module, not a logic bug).
commands.name: red-pytest

commands.observed_result: 29 failed, 1 passed. All failures were the same AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute '__dict__' inside dataclasses._process_class, caused by the test helper's dynamic import via importlib.util not registering the module in sys.modules before exec_module -- dataclasses resolves postponed annotations via sys.modules[cls.__module__]. This was a test-infrastructure bug, not a production-logic failure; fixed by registering sys.modules[spec.name] = module before spec.loader.exec_module(module) in the test file's _import_module() helper.
commands.name: green-pytest-diagnose

commands.purpose: Green gate for the full coordinator callback executor test suite after fixing the test import helper.
commands.observed_result: 30 passed in 0.06s. Covers AC1 (explicit-parameter identity, malformed workspace/surface/run-id/event-name validation, no environment-variable identity interface), AC2 (first delivery invokes cmux with the correct ref, workspace-only fallback, repeated delivery is a no-op, distinct events and distinct run ids both still deliver, a 32-way ThreadPoolExecutor concurrent-duplicate test invokes cmux exactly once), AC3 (malformed/command-injection-style refs rejected before any command runs, missing cmux binary fails visibly and rolls back its lock so a later retry still delivers, a non-zero cmux exit fails visibly and also allows retry, stale/mismatched state file content is detected and rejected without invoking cmux, an unwritable state path fails visibly, and main() reports errors on stderr with a non-zero exit and no traceback), and AC4 (no-callback behavior: neither --workspace nor --surface supplied exits 0 without invoking cmux, both via the internal deliver_callback() API and via main()).
commands.name: green-pytest

commands.purpose: Syntax/compile sanity check on both new files.
commands.name: py-compile

commands.purpose: Confirm this bead's commits touch only the two new files and do not modify bin/cld, bin/cdx, or any other existing file (scope-out check per bead instructions).
commands.observed_result: 2 files changed, 803 insertions(+): scripts/coordinator_callback.py (390 lines) and tests/test_coordinator_callback_executor.py (413 lines). No other files touched.
commands.name: scope-check

skipped_checks.reason: Out of scope by prompt ("Run ONLY tests for files you changed"); this bead added exactly one new test file and one new production module with no dependents yet, so tests/test_coordinator_callback_executor.py is the complete regression surface.

skipped_checks.check: Wiring scripts/coordinator_callback.py into bin/cld or bin/cdx
skipped_checks.reason: Explicit Scope-Out per bead instructions -- lifecycle wiring belongs to sibling beads CL-gzvu (cld) and CL-eqiq (cdx).

Per python/dependency-injection.md, all subprocess/which calls in the delivery path go through an injectable CommandRunner; tests use MockCommandRunner exclusively and never shell out to a real cmux binary, by design.

skipped_checks.check: Metrics logging to canonical beads metrics database
skipped_checks.reason: Attempted per the Metrics Logging instructions using the insert_agent_call helper; treated as best-effort per the bead's own instruction ("do not block completion on this").

artifacts.description: Command evidence ledger for CL-t32e red and green gates.
